behavior_in_task_description.reason: The instruction explicitly names all file paths (rake_tasks/rust.rake, .github/workflows/pre-release.yml), exact literal strings (Rake::Task['rust:update'].reenable, Rake::Task['rust:update'].invoke, 'Repin cargo immediately after updating the version', CARGO_BAZEL_REPIN, mid-evaluation/file-hash conflict), ordering requirements, task block containment, and ruby -c syntax check. The tests for workflow simplification (test_workflow_simplified_command) check for absence of a specific old pattern,

no_solution_leakage.reason: The instruction explicitly states the exact strings to add: 'Rake::Task["rust:update"].reenable', 'Rake::Task["rust:update"].invoke', the exact comment text 'Repin cargo immediately after updating the version', and that CARGO_BAZEL_REPIN and mid-evaluation must appear in comments. This is telling the agent exactly WHAT to write, not just describing what's broken. Section 'Required Implementation Details' is essentially a line-by-line specification of the solution.

solution_uniqueness_guard.reason: The tests assert exact literal strings like 'Repin cargo immediately after updating the version', exact method calls 'Rake::Task["rust:update"].reenable' and 'Rake::Task["rust:update"].invoke'. An alternative correct fix could use system('CARGO_BAZEL_REPIN=true bazel fetch @crates//:all') directly, or use Rake.application['rust:update'].invoke, or use a different comment wording. These would all be correct but would fail the tests.

tests_verify_behavior_not_text.reason: The f2p tests (test_rust_rake_file_contains_reenable, test_rust_rake_file_contains_invoke_after_version, test_rust_rake_has_comment_explaining_fix, test_rust_rake_comment_mentions_cargo_bazel_repin, test_rust_rake_comment_mentions_mid_evaluation_conflict, test_reenable_comes_before_invoke, test_invoke_in_version_task_block) are ALL string-matching tests using 'assert X in open(path).read()'. While there are some p2p tests that run bazel/ruby commands, the core f2p tests exclusively check for tex

test_not_tautological.reason: All f2p tests check for the presence of specific strings in the file. An agent could simply append a comment block containing all the required strings without understanding the bug at all: '# Repin cargo immediately after updating the version CARGO_BAZEL_REPIN mid-evaluation file-hash conflict\nRake::Task["rust:update"].reenable\nRake::Task["rust:update"].invoke'. This would pass all f2p tests without any real fix implementation.

behavior_in_tests.reason: All 8 required implementation details from the instruction are tested: reenable string, invoke string, exact comment phrase, CARGO_BAZEL_REPIN mention, mid-evaluation mention, ordering, block containment, and Ruby syntax validity.

informative_test_structure.reason: Tests are well-named with descriptive docstrings, clearly separated into f2p and p2p categories with comments, and each test has a specific purpose explained in its name and assertion message.

structured_data_schema.reason: This task involves editing configuration/build files, not producing structured data output.

no_typos.reason: File paths (rake_tasks/rust.rake, .github/workflows/pre-release.yml), command names (ruby -c, bazel build), and variable names are all correct and consistent.

instruction_no_hint_leakage.reason: The instruction names the exact file (rake_tasks/rust.rake), the exact task block ('task :version ... do ... end'), and specifies the exact strings to insert including exact comment text. This goes far beyond identifying the buggy area — it specifies the complete solution content.

config_edit_scope_bounded.reason: The gold patch makes localized changes: adding ~12 lines at the end of a task block in rust.rake and changing one line in pre-release.yml. Neither contradicts other tier-1 files.
